1_validate_design.rationale: The GKE worker deployment exposes pool and host metadata but not a complete libpq credential. The existing NoETL credential named pg_k8s already carries db_host, db_port, db_name, db_user, and db_password, so each render_* step receives workload.pg_auth as input and resolves it through noetl.worker.secrets.fetch_credential_by_key. This preserves NoETL's credential boundary and avoids coupling the playbook to partial pod env vars.
checks: noetl CLI on this machine does not expose a validate subcommand; model validation was used instead

regressions_found: The local Amadeus test API path continues to return upstream failures, so all four user-facing branch smokes tailed at render_amadeus_failure. This did not block the round because the friendly-error renderer is one of the five audited render steps and now records render_kind for the originating intent.

The /travel canvas smoke rendered the execution JSON in the transcript while the persisted playbook.completed event and audit table both contain render_type=app:column. This appears to be an existing GUI projection/display behavior rather than an audit-side-effect regression.
notes: The runtime comment from round 6 was replaced with the implemented pattern: audit work lives inside the render tail and cannot clobber execution.result.render.
No trailing kind:postgres step was reintroduced.
No repos/noetl, repos/gui, or repos/e2e changes were made.
deferred_followups: Investigate why the local /travel canvas transcript showed raw execution JSON instead of the widget despite persisted render events.
Continue tracking the Amadeus test API 500 behavior separately from the audit side-effect pattern.
